home.tagline: Designing the next generation of lithium-ion and solid-state battery materials.
home.hero_eyebrow: EEML · Energy & Environmental Materials Lab · Gachon University
home.research_intro: From dry electrode processing to solid-state systems and novel energy conversion — we build at the boundary of materials chemistry and energy.

contact.title: Contact
contact.join_us_desc: We welcome motivated students passionate about Li-ion and solid-state battery research.

join.title: Join Us
join.lead_title: We're looking for students to join us.
join.lead_desc: EEML (Energy & Environmental Materials Lab) at Gachon University is always recruiting graduate students (MS/PhD) and undergraduate researchers interested in solid-state batteries, Li metal anodes, battery materials, and electrochemical devices. We value curiosity and willingness to learn over your specific background (chemistry, chemical engineering, materials, mechanical, physics, and more are all welcome).
join.step1_desc: Send an email to Prof. Dongsoo Lee following the contact guide below. A short self-introduction and your CV (or transcript) is enough.
join.step2_desc: A casual meeting to discuss ongoing research, lab life, and your interests. Feel free to ask anything.
join.step3_desc: If possible, we recommend experiencing the lab first as an undergraduate researcher (intern). It's the best way to check whether the research topics and lab culture fit you. Duration and activities are arranged during the interview.
join.step4_desc: Once both sides are confident, you apply to the Gachon University graduate admissions (spring/fall). We'll guide you through the schedule and process.
join.contact_guide_desc: Please include: 1) your name, affiliation, and year, 2) research interests, 3) CV or transcript attached, 4) 2-3 lines on why you want to join. Substance matters more than format — tell us in your own words why battery research, and why our lab.
join.faq1_q: Can I try the lab first as an undergraduate researcher (intern)?
join.faq1_a: Yes. Both semester and vacation periods are possible; duration and activities are arranged during the interview. If you're considering graduate school, we strongly recommend an internship period first.
join.faq2_a: Graduate student stipends are provided according to lab policy and participating research projects. Details are explained transparently during the interview.
join.faq3_q: Can students from other universities or majors apply?
join.faq3_a: Absolutely. We welcome related majors — chemistry, chemical engineering, materials, mechanical, physics, and more — and applicants from other universities. No prior battery research experience required.
join.faq4_a: Through the interview and internship period, we consider both your interests and the lab's ongoing projects. Check the Research page to explore our research areas in advance.
